official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Construct, write, decode, and observe the container id, state id, slot, and empty item body
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound container_set_slot frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns container_set_slot immediately after container_set_data.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the container_set_slot id to ClientboundContainerSetSlotPacket.
The official container_set_slot body is containerId, stateId, slot, and itemStack in STREAM_CODEC order.

The fixture uses the official ClientboundContainerSetSlotPacket constructor with numeric fields and ItemStack.EMPTY, so it does not require an initialized Menu, screen, Level, inventory, item registry entry, component registry, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded container id, decoded state id, decoded slot, decoded empty-item flag, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.

This contract does not prove non-empty ItemStack/component registry handling, menu lifecycle behavior, inventory state, initialized game state, runtime Play entry, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer non-empty ItemStack/component registry handling, menu lifecycle behavior, inventory state, initialized game state, runtime Play entry, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
